messages.content:
What is Accenture's most important advantage when it comes to 5G and edge computing?


the number of custom-built cell sites we have implemented


our market share in the fields of cell networks and computing


the amount of funds we have invested in cutting-edge technology


our extensive experience in dealing with disruptive technologies


I don't know this yet.

As an AI language model, I don't have access to real-time information or company data. However, based on general industry knowledge, Accenture's most important advantage when it comes to 5G and edge computing could possibly be their extensive experience in dealing with disruptive technologies. Accenture is known for its expertise and consulting services in various technological domains, so their experience in navigating and implementing disruptive technologies could give them an edge in the 5G and edge computing space. However, it is advisable to refer to official sources or the latest information from Accenture for the most accurate and up-to-date answer.

What happens after the initial rollout of a new network generation?


Development of the network is frozen until the rollout of the next network generation.


All devices created to work with the previous network generation must upgrade or lose service.


The previous network generation will be deactivated after a transition period of a few months.


The new network generation will continue to be developed and enhanced over its lifetime.


I don't know this yet.

After the initial rollout of a new network generation, the new network will continue to be developed and enhanced over its lifetime. This means that improvements, optimizations, and additional features can be added to the network infrastructure as technology advances and new requirements emerge. However, specific transitions or deactivations of previous network generations may vary depending on industry standards, service providers, and regional regulations. It is always recommended to refer to relevant sources and industry updates for the most accurate and current information regarding network transitions and developments.

An air purifier manufacturer is looking for ideas on using edge computing to enhance its products and services.

Which approach would make use of edge computing technologies?


a local support staff based in the customer's geographic location for more rapid responses


a device in the customer's home that detects air quality levels and sends alerts when needed


a scheduling feature that activates a customer's air purifier at a defined time each day


an automated program that can interpret customer service requests and assign agents accordingly


I don't know this yet.

The approach that would make use of edge computing technologies in this scenario would be "a device in the customer's home that detects air quality levels and sends alerts when needed." Edge computing involves processing data locally on devices or edge servers, rather than sending it to a centralized cloud or data center. In this case, the air purifier's device would utilize edge computing technologies to analyze and interpret air quality data locally, enabling real-time monitoring and alerting without relying on external servers or internet connectivity. This approach enhances efficiency, reduces latency, and ensures timely responses for air quality monitoring and alerts.

What underlying concept is edge computing based on?


transmitting the maximum amount of data over a single connection


moving computing resources closer to the source or user of data


delivering files to end users at the fastest possible speeds


using beamforming to improve the efficiency of a network


I don't know this yet.

The underlying concept of edge computing is "moving computing resources closer to the source or user of data." Edge computing aims to bring data processing and computation closer to the devices or sensors where the data is generated or consumed, rather than relying on centralized cloud servers or data centers. By placing computing resources at the edge of the network, edge computing reduces latency, improves response times, enhances privacy and security, and enables real-time processing and analysis of data. This concept is especially relevant for applications where low latency and high reliability are crucial, such as IoT devices, real-time analytics, and autonomous systems.

An internet service provider needs to deliver 5G connectivity services to a customer residing on the 30th floor of a tall building. To improve reception of the 5G signal at that height, the provider uses a technology that enables an antenna to reconfigure and deliver better signal range and bandwidth.

What is the name for this technology?


platooning


beamforming


massive MIMO


pixel streaming


I don't know this yet.

The technology that enables an antenna to reconfigure and deliver better signal range and bandwidth in this scenario is called "beamforming." Beamforming is a technique used in wireless communication, including 5G, where multiple antenna elements work together to create a focused beam or signal. By directing the signal towards a specific direction or user, beamforming can improve signal strength, range, and overall performance, especially in challenging environments such as tall buildings or areas with obstacles. This technology helps ensure more reliable and efficient connectivity for users residing on higher floors of buildings.

What is a benefit of 5G mmWave technology?


It has low frequency, allowing it to transmit over longer ranges.


It can transmit a large amount of information with low latency.


It easily passes through obstacles such as walls and trees.


It provides coverage to low-density rural and suburban areas.


I don't know this yet.

A benefit of 5G mmWave technology is that it can transmit a large amount of information with low latency. The mmWave spectrum used in 5G networks offers significantly higher bandwidth compared to lower frequency bands. This allows for faster data transfer rates and enables the transmission of large amounts of data, such as high-definition video streaming, virtual reality applications, and other data-intensive services. Additionally, with its low latency, 5G mmWave technology supports real-time responsiveness, making it suitable for applications that require instant feedback and quick interactions, such as autonomous vehicles and robotics.

A homeowner uses a smart assistant to set the house alarm, get package delivery updates, and set timers on the outdoor lights.

What are these functions an example of?


blockchain


internet of things


massive MIMO


virtualization


I don't know this yet.

The functions described in the scenario are an example of the "Internet of Things" (IoT). The IoT refers to the network of physical devices, vehicles, appliances, and other objects embedded with sensors, software, and connectivity. These devices can communicate and exchange data with each other over the internet, enabling them to be remotely monitored, controlled, and automate various tasks. In the scenario, the smart assistant acts as a central hub that connects and controls different IoT devices such as the alarm system, package delivery updates, and outdoor lights. This interconnected network of devices enhances convenience and automation in a smart home setup.

Which situation would benefit the most by using edge computing?


an offshore oil rig needs to more efficiently process data


a theater wants to record performances for its website


an IT department wants to increase the capacity of its central server


a clothing company wants to track customer preferences


I don't know this yet.

The situation that would benefit the most from using edge computing is when an offshore oil rig needs to more efficiently process data. Offshore oil rigs are typically located in remote areas with limited network connectivity. By utilizing edge computing, data processing can be performed locally on the rig itself, reducing the need to transmit large amounts of data to a centralized server. Edge computing enables real-time analytics and decision-making at the edge of the network, improving efficiency, reducing latency, and ensuring timely responses. This is particularly crucial in industries like oil and gas, where immediate data processing and analysis are critical for operational safety and optimization.

Why are 5G mmWave signals more likely to be blocked by physical barriers such as walls, buildings, and trees?


Its signals transmit less information than older signals.


Its signals are transmitted at higher frequencies.


Its signals are transmitted at lower frequencies.


It is designed to work only within a single building.


I don't know this yet.

5G mmWave signals are more likely to be blocked by physical barriers such as walls, buildings, and trees because they are transmitted at higher frequencies. The higher the frequency of a signal, the shorter its wavelength, and the more it is prone to attenuation and blockage by obstacles. The mmWave spectrum used in 5G has significantly higher frequencies compared to older wireless technologies, which allows for greater bandwidth and faster data transfer. However, these higher frequencies cannot penetrate physical barriers as effectively as lower frequencies. Thus, mmWave signals have more difficulty passing through obstacles like walls, buildings, and trees, resulting in a limited range and reduced signal penetration compared to lower frequency 5G bands.

What does latency indicate?


the number of devices currently connected to the network


the amount of energy needed to establish a network connection


the number of MIMO antennas used to connect a device


the amount of lag time between an action and response


I don't know this yet.

Latency refers to the amount of lag time or delay between when an action is initiated and when a response is received. In the context of networks, it specifically indicates the time taken for data to travel from the source to the destination and for a response to be sent back. Lower latency means a faster response time, while higher latency indicates more delay in communication. Latency can be affected by various factors such as network congestion, distance, processing time, and the efficiency of the communication medium. It is an important consideration for real-time applications and services that require immediate feedback, such as online gaming, video conferencing, and autonomous systems.

Edge computing is often referred to as a "topology."

What does this term describe?


a synonym that can be used interchangeably with “technology“


a set of programming languages used to develop an application


how much processing power an application consumes


where the different parts of an application are located


I don't know this yet.

The term "topology" when referring to edge computing does not describe a set of programming languages, processing power, or a synonym for technology. Instead, it describes where the different parts of an application or computing infrastructure are located or distributed. In the context of edge computing, topology refers to the arrangement or structure of edge devices, servers, and network components that enable processing and storage of data closer to the source or user. It encompasses the spatial distribution and connectivity of these components in a networked system, including their relationships and how they interact with each other. The topology in edge computing defines how and where the computing resources are distributed to optimize performance, latency, and reliability of applications.

A large offshore oil platform needs to transmit signals between different devices on the platform with high reliability and low latency. However, they are unable to cover the platform with their existing Wi-Fi connections.

What is the best solution for the platform in this scenario?


routing all their web traffic through the company headquarters


building new 5G cell sites between the platform and the shore


setting up a private 5G network on the platform itself


adding a satellite link to the existing Wi-Fi network


I don't know this yet.

The best solution for the offshore oil platform in this scenario would be setting up a private 5G network on the platform itself. 

Since the Wi-Fi connections are insufficient for covering the platform, a private 5G network can provide reliable and low-latency connectivity between devices on the platform. With a private 5G network, the platform can have dedicated and localized wireless communication infrastructure that can cater to their specific needs without relying on external connections or infrastructure. This allows for better control, security, and performance in transmitting signals between different devices on the platform.

What is a recent innovation in edge computing enhanced by 5G?


connecting applications to a telecom company's central data center


placing computing resources physically inside the network infrastructure


transferring computing resources between geographic regions


routing long-distance phone calls through a network of cell towers


I don't know this yet.

A recent innovation in edge computing enhanced by 5G is the concept of placing computing resources physically inside the network infrastructure, commonly referred to as "edge cloud" or "cloudlets." 

With this approach, computing infrastructure, including servers, storage, and other resources, is deployed at the edge of the network, closer to the end-users or devices generating data. These edge cloud nodes provide low-latency processing and storage capabilities, enabling faster access to applications, services, and data. The combination of edge computing and 5G networks allows for more efficient and dynamic resource allocation, reducing latency and enabling real-time processing of data at the edge. This innovation has significant implications for applications such as augmented reality, autonomous systems, and IoT devices that require immediate and responsive computing capabilities.

Why does it take so long for each generation of wireless communication technology to become broadly available?


Consumers are resistant to change and have little demand for greater bandwidth.


Network infrastructure for the previous generation must be shut down before the rollout of the next generation.


Devices that support a new generation are typically not available until years after the network is fully rolled out.


Network infrastructure and cell towers need to be upgraded to support next generation hardware.


I don't know this yet.

The main reason it takes so long for each generation of wireless communication technology to become broadly available is that network infrastructure and cell towers need to be upgraded to support the next generation hardware. 

Implementing a new wireless generation involves significant investments and upgrades in the existing network infrastructure to accommodate the technological advancements. This includes updating cell towers, antennas, and backhaul infrastructure, as well as ensuring compatibility with new transmission protocols and frequencies. Additionally, network operators need to plan and deploy the upgraded infrastructure gradually while minimizing disruption to existing services. The process of testing, upgrading, and expanding network infrastructure across a large geographic area can be time-consuming and resource-intensive, leading to a delay in the broad availability of the new wireless technology.

A health care client is looking to create wearable devices that constantly monitor the health of critical patients, suggest medications, and alert a doctor in case of an emergency.

How do 5G and edge computing work together to make this possible?


by storing patient information in a blockchain


by routing all signals through a single central server


by turning off devices that are used less frequently


by transmitting critical information with minimal delay


I don't know this yet.

5G and edge computing work together to make wearable devices that constantly monitor critical patients, suggest medications, and alert doctors in case of emergencies possible by transmitting critical information with minimal delay. 

5G networks provide fast and reliable communication capabilities with low latency, allowing real-time transmission of data from the wearable devices to the medical professionals and systems. This enables timely monitoring, analysis, and response to critical health data. 

Edge computing complements 5G by processing and analyzing the data locally on edge devices or edge servers near the patient. By leveraging edge computing, the data from wearable devices can be processed closer to the source, reducing latency and enabling immediate actions and alerts. This ensures that time-sensitive information is processed and acted upon rapidly, enhancing the effectiveness and responsiveness of the healthcare system.

Together, 5G and edge computing enable seamless and real-time monitoring, intelligent analysis, and immediate communication for critical patient care in the healthcare domain.
